failure_surface: Agent failed to implement the case-sensitive conditional matching requirement where calculations should only apply to rows where column A values match cell J2's value.
root_cause: The agent misinterpreted the instruction and applied unconditional profit calculations (B-C-E-G) to all rows 2-5, completely ignoring the requirement to conditionally calculate only where Type column matches J2's value in a case-sensitive manner.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When an instruction specifies conditional calculations based on matching column values (especially with case-sensitivity requirements), the agent must construct formulas or logic that incorporate these conditional filters rather than applying calculations unconditionally across all target cells.
evidence: Instruction explicitly states: 'evaluate this calculation based where column A matches the case sensitive values in J2'
Agent's verification output shows formulas were written unconditionally to K2:K5 without any reference to cell J2 or Type column comparison
Final formulas generated were: =IF(ISBLANK(B2),0,B2)-IF(ISBLANK(C2),0,C2)-IF(ISBLANK(E2),0,E2)-IF(ISBLANK(G2),0,G2) - no conditional matching against J2 present
Agent acknowledged column A values ('retail', 'FLEET', 'retail', 'retail') and column J2 value ('RETAIL') but never incorporated this comparison into the solution

memory_items.title: Conditional Calculation Requirements
memory_items.lesson: When instructions specify calculations should apply conditionally based on row data matching (e.g., 'where column X matches value in Y'), formulas must include this conditional logic rather than applying unconditionally.
memory_items.generalization: Always identify if a task requires conditional row selection before applying transformations. Parse phrases like 'based where', 'when column matches', 'only if' as triggers for conditional formulas (IF, FILTER, VLOOKUP with conditions).

memory_items.title: Case-Sensitive Text Matching Awareness
memory_items.lesson: Explicitly stated case-sensitivity requirements in instructions require careful attention - Excel's standard comparisons are often case-insensitive unless EXACT() function is used.
memory_items.generalization: When instructions mention 'case sensitive' matching, verify whether built-in functions support this behavior and use appropriate functions (EXACT, or case-sensitive comparison methods) to honor the requirement.

memory_items.title: Reference Cell Dependencies
memory_items.lesson: Instructions referencing specific cells (like 'J2') for comparison values must have those cells included as fixed references ($J$2) in formulas to maintain proper dependency tracking.
memory_items.generalization: Any formula referencing a comparison/value source cell should lock that reference appropriately. Verify all named cells mentioned in instructions are actually incorporated into the implemented solution.

edits.content:
## Conditional Row Selection
- When instructions specify calculations apply 'where' certain conditions are met (e.g., 'where column A matches J2'), formulas must embed conditional logic rather than applying universally.
- Identify trigger phrases: 'only if', 'when', 'based where', 'filtered by' indicate conditional transformation requirements.
- Case-sensitive matching requires explicit functions (e.g., EXACT in Excel) as standard comparisons ignore case.
- Any referenced control cells (like $J$2) must appear as fixed references in generated formulas to maintain dependencies.
